title: Introductio to geobr (R)
text: Following steps , can use together geobr sister package censobr map proportion households connected sewage network Brazilian municipalities First, need download households data Brazilian census using read_households() function. Now ’re going () group observations municipality, (b) get number households connected sewage network, (c) calculate proportion households connected, (d) collect results. Now need download geometries Brazilian municipalities geobr, merge spatial data estimates map results.
code: library(censobr) library(arrow) #>  #> Attaching package: 'arrow' #> The following object is masked from 'package:utils': #>  #>     timestamp  hs <- read_households(   year = 2010,    showProgress = FALSE   ) #> ℹ Downloading data and storing it locally for future use. esg <- hs |>          collect() |>         group_by(code_muni) |>                                             # (a)         summarize(rede = sum(V0010[which(V0207=='1')]),                    # (b)                   total = sum(V0010)) |>                                   # (b)         mutate(cobertura = rede / total) |>                                # (c)         collect()                                                          # (d)  head(esg) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   code_muni     rede  total cobertura #>       <int>    <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1   1100015     0     7443.   0       #> 2   1100023   182.   27654.   0.00660 #> 3   1100031     0     1979.   0       #> 4   1100049 10019.   24413.   0.410   #> 5   1100056     5.81  5399    0.00108 #> 6   1100064    28.9   6013.   0.00480 # download municipality geometries muni_sf <- geobr::read_municipality(   year = 2010,   showProgress = FALSE   ) #> ℹ Using year/date 2010  # merge data esg_sf <- left_join(muni_sf, esg, by = 'code_muni')  # plot map ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = esg_sf, aes(fill = cobertura), color=NA) +   labs(title = "Share of households connected to a sewage network") +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Greens", direction = 1,                         name='Share of\nhouseholds',                         labels = scales::percent) +   theme_void()

title: Download spatial data of urbanized areas in Brazil — read_urban_area
text: year Numeric. Year data YYYY format. defaults NULL reads data latest year available. code_muni 7-digit code municipality. code_muni = "" (Default), function downloads data available country. Alternatively, two-digit state code two-letter uppercase abbreviation state passed (e.g. 33 "RJ"), data state downloaded. Municipality codes can consulted geobr::lookup_muni() function. simplified Logic FALSE TRUE, indicating whether function return data set 'original' spatial resolution data set 'simplified' geometry. Defaults TRUE. spatial analysis statistics users set simplified = FALSE. Borders simplified removing vertices borders using st_simplify{sf} preserving topology dTolerance 100. output String. Whether output "sf" object loaded memory (default), "arrow" arrow data set. showProgress Logical. Defaults TRUE display progress bar. cache Logical. Whether function read data cached locally, faster. Defaults cache = TRUE. default, geobr stores data files temporary directory exists within R session. cache = FALSE, function download data overwrite local file. verbose logical. TRUE (default), function prints informative messages shows download progress bar. FALSE, function silent.

text: New functions read_favela() data favelas urban communities (source: IBGE) Closes #387. read_polling_places() data pooling places (source: TSE) Closes #184 #242. read_quilombola_lands() data officialy recognized quilombola lands (source: INCRA) Closes #242. remove_islands() remove islands Brazil. Closes #412. Breaking changes year date arguments can longer NULL; must explicitly specified. change intentional meant encourage users mindful historical changes data. geom column renamed geometry consistency read_health_region() completely rewritten allow users return detailed output needed Functions like read_schools() read_health_facilities() now use combination official spatial coordinates coordinates found using {geocodebr} package improve spatial accuracy. See documentation functions. function lookup_muni() now year parameter. Closes #401. function data read_comparable_areas() going major changes. now, function temporarily suspended. year available far functions read_urban_concentrations() read_pop_arrangements()2010, 2015. Closes #290 functions new argument output, allow users choose whether functions return "sf" memory (default) "arrow" table. functions new argument verbose. TRUE (default), function prints informative messages shows download progress bar. FALSE, function silent. Closes #400. function list_geobr() now boolean argument wide, users can choose whether output presented wide long format. function lookup_muni() now uses probabilistic match find municipality names users might input typos. Closes #406. following functions now include column code_state allow users filter data directly function call: read_indigenous_land(), read_metro_area(), read_pop_arrangements() read_urban_concentrations(). following functions now include column code_muni allow users filter data directly function call: read_disaster_risk_area(), read_health_facilities(), read_neighborhood(), read_statistical_grid() read_schools().
